when_to_use: Use present simple for things you do regularly, habits, routines, facts, and things that are always true.
when_to_use_ar: استخدم المضارع البسيط للأشياء اللي تسويها بشكل منتظم، العادات، الروتين، والحقائق.
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: Arabic has a different verb conjugation system. In English present simple, the verb does NOT change for I/you/we/they — it stays in its base form.

common_errors.explanation: Don't add 'am/is/are' before main verbs. Just: I + verb.
Present simple = base form. No -ing for habits.
For habits, use the base verb alone. 'am' + verb = different tense.

practice_fill_blank.hint: Base form — no change for I
Base form — no change for we
Base form — no change for they
Base form — no change for you
Base form — no change for I

negative_form.note: don't = do not (always use don't in speaking)

quick_rule: For I/you/we/they: just use the base verb. No changes. No -s. No -ing. No 'am/is'.
quick_rule_ar: مع أنا/أنت/نحن/هم: استخدم الفعل بشكله الأساسي. بدون تغيير.
connect_to_speaking: When describing your daily routine, every sentence uses this pattern: 'I wake up. I eat. I go. I study. I sleep.'
